no-restricted-globals.name: event
no-restricted-globals.message: Use a local event parameter instead

no-restricted-globals.name: fdescribe
no-restricted-globals.message: Focused test global leaked into scanned code

no-restricted-globals.name: fit
no-restricted-globals.message: Focused test global leaked into scanned code

no-restricted-globals.name: SharedArrayBuffer
no-restricted-globals.message: SharedArrayBuffer can enable Spectre-class attacks; verify the threat model

no-restricted-properties.message: document.write is an XSS sink; use DOM methods or templating instead
document.writeln is an XSS sink
Setting document.domain weakens same-origin isolation
Deprecated metaprogramming API; review for prototype pollution or sandbox escape patterns
Deprecated metaprogramming API; review for prototype pollution or sandbox escape patterns
Deprecated metaprogramming API; review for prototype pollution or sandbox escape patterns
Deprecated metaprogramming API; review for prototype pollution or sandbox escape patterns
Object.assign with untrusted input can enable prototype pollution; validate merge sources
Buffer.allocUnsafe can expose stale memory; prefer Buffer.alloc
Buffer.allocUnsafeSlow can expose stale memory; prefer Buffer.alloc
crypto.createCipher is deprecated and unsafe; use createCipheriv with a random IV
crypto.createDecipher is deprecated and unsafe; use createDecipheriv
Use crypto.randomBytes instead; pseudoRandomBytes is not suitable for new code
Math.random is not cryptographically secure; do not use it for secrets or tokens
process.exit short-circuits cleanup and can hide security-sensitive shutdown behavior
child_process.exec is command-injection prone; prefer execFile with a fixed binary and args array
child_process.execSync is command-injection prone; prefer execFileSync with fixed args
vm.runInNewContext executes dynamic code; treat all inputs as hostile
vm.runInThisContext executes dynamic code; treat all inputs as hostile
vm.runInContext executes dynamic code; treat all inputs as hostile
vm.compileFunction compiles dynamic code; validate all code strings and context objects

no-restricted-syntax.message: Setting innerHTML is an XSS sink; prefer textContent or a sanitiser
Setting outerHTML is an XSS sink
Setting srcdoc injects HTML into an iframe; sanitise content first
insertAdjacentHTML is an XSS sink; sanitise content first
dangerouslySetInnerHTML is an XSS sink; sanitise content first
setTimeout with a string argument is equivalent to eval()
setInterval with a string argument is equivalent to eval()
Dynamic require() can become code injection if user input reaches the module path
Dynamic import() can become code injection if user input reaches the module path
Dynamic RegExp construction can become ReDoS if untrusted input controls the pattern
RegExp.compile is deprecated and unsafe with dynamic input
Direct location.href assignment should be reviewed for open redirects
Setting location.href should be reviewed for open redirects
location.assign should be reviewed for open redirects
location.replace should be reviewed for open redirects
window.open should be reviewed for open redirect or phishing flows
postMessage should use a specific targetOrigin rather than '*'
Setting href should be reviewed for javascript: URLs and user-controlled redirects
Setting src should be reviewed when the value is user-controlled
Setting form action should be reviewed for user-controlled redirects
Setting formaction should be reviewed for user-controlled redirects
Dynamic fetch URL should be reviewed for SSRF or credential-leak flows
DOMParser.parseFromString(..., 'text/html') parses attacker-controlled HTML; sanitise first
fs.readFileSync should be reviewed for path traversal when the path is user-controlled
fs.readFile should be reviewed for path traversal when the path is user-controlled
fs.writeFileSync should be reviewed for arbitrary file write when the path is user-controlled
fs.writeFile should be reviewed for arbitrary file write when the path is user-controlled
fs.createReadStream should be reviewed for path traversal when the path is user-controlled
fs.createWriteStream should be reviewed for arbitrary file write when the path is user-controlled
fs.unlink should be reviewed for arbitrary file delete when the path is user-controlled
fs.unlinkSync should be reviewed for arbitrary file delete when the path is user-controlled
res.sendFile should be reviewed for path traversal when the file path is not fixed
res.download should be reviewed for path traversal when the file path is not fixed
SQL query built from a template literal should use parameter binding instead
SQL query built from string concatenation should use parameter binding instead
Deserialization should be reviewed for untrusted input
unserialize-style APIs are dangerous with untrusted input
new Function() is equivalent to eval() and should not receive untrusted input
Sending raw headers should be reviewed for header injection
Dynamic HTTP header values should be reviewed for CRLF injection
Dynamic redirect targets should be reviewed for open redirects
MD5 is cryptographically broken; use SHA-256 or better
SHA-1 is deprecated for security-sensitive hashing; use SHA-256 or better
HMAC-MD5 is deprecated; prefer SHA-256 or better
HMAC-SHA1 is deprecated; prefer SHA-256 or better
rejectUnauthorized: false disables TLS verification and should be treated as a security finding
secure: false weakens transport protections; verify this is not a production cookie or session setting
httpOnly: false exposes cookies to JavaScript and increases XSS impact
Avoid 'as any' in security-sensitive paths; it can hide unsafe input handling
Avoid casting to 'any' in security-sensitive paths; it can hide unsafe input handling
Non-null assertions can bypass validation and error handling on attacker-controlled data
Using 'satisfies any' defeats useful type checks in security-relevant code
